mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and capacity utilization, which translates into higher expected corporate revenues and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window signals accelerating labor market deterioration that reduces household income growth and consumer spending expectations, generating negative S&P 500 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE has risen >0.3pp over the preceding 3 months during any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously elevated FEDFUNDS and declining RSAFS identify a monetary tightening cycle hitting consumer demand; this joint condition compresses corporate margins and equity valuations beyond either factor alone, producing negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and RSAFS fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.05) in predicting negative 20-day returns, or the joint signal underperforms the single-factor RSAFS model by more than 2% annualized over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in RSAFS predicts positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns exclusively in low-inflation regimes, because demand expansion without inflationary pressure supports real profit margins rather than triggering offsetting monetary tightening.
failure_condition: During low-inflation regimes, positive RSAFS growth fails to predict positive 20-day S&P 500 returns with an information coefficient greater than 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPIAUCSL print exceeding its trailing three-month average by more than 0.1 percentage points triggers an upward repricing of inflation risk and real discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 5-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change exceeds prior 3-month average by > 0.1pp
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL surprises above its 3-month trend, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in RSAFS signals durable consumer demand momentum that feeds into revenue and earnings surprises over the following quarter, producing positive S&P 500 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter RSAFS growth is positive but 60-day forward S&P 500 returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 3%, a concurrent month-over-month decline in INDPRO indicates that restrictive monetary policy is successfully slowing industrial activity; this interaction amplifies downside earnings revisions and produces negative S&P 500 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: In months where FEDFUNDS exceeds 3%, INDPRO declines fail to produce negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of cases, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP (GDPC1) exceeding its trailing four-quarter average by more than 1% signals above-trend economic momentum that causes broad earnings estimate revisions upward, producing positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 exceeds its 4-quarter trend by >1%, 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 0% with p > 0.05 from baseline) over a 5-year rolling window.
